human_verification.notes: Validated child-care-specific nutrition boundaries, CACFP and Head Start source quality, dietary-accommodation evidence handling, blocker and closure behavior, adjacent specialist routing, and validated packaging completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to child-nutrition coordination, evidence checks, blocker tracking, routing, and closure confirmation in child care and family services operations.
Authority sources anchor the pack in USDA CACFP guidance, Head Start nutrition rules, and recognized early-childhood nutrition and safety standards.
Scenarios validate missing prerequisites, conflicting owners, adjacent-lane routing, child-care-specific in-scope work, and low-confidence escalation.
The package prevents generic food-service behavior by requiring meal counts, attendance, accommodation records, infant-feeding context, and sponsor or tenant workflow facts.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of meal-service, accommodation, and sponsor-review records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
